title: Fix CSS Grid Auto-Flow with Dynamic Row Height Calculation

statement.markdown: Write a CSS code that fixes the grid auto-flow with dynamic row height calculation. Given a grid container with CSS grid properties, the code should calculate the row height based on the maximum height of its items and adjust the row height accordingly. The grid should auto-flow with a minimum of 2 rows and maximum of 5 rows.
statement.inputFormat: A CSS string representing the grid container and its items.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS code with the grid auto-flow and dynamic row height calculation.
statement.notes: The CSS code should be concise and readable.

examples.explanation: The grid auto-flow should be fixed to auto-flow with dynamic row height calculation.
The grid auto-flow should be fixed to auto-flow with dynamic row height calculation.

hints.text: The grid-auto-rows property should be set to max-content to allow the row height to be calculated dynamically.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Grid Auto-Flow
editorial.intuition: To fix the grid auto-flow, we need to adjust the grid-auto-rows property to allow the row height to be calculated dynamically.
editorial.bruteForce: We can manually calculate the row height for each item and update the grid-auto-rows property accordingly.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the max-content value for the grid-auto-rows property to allow the row height to be calculated automatically.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to set the grid-auto-rows property to max-content to allow the row height to be calculated dynamically.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected CSS code will auto-flow with dynamic row height calculation.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Set the grid-auto-rows property to max-content to allow the row height to be calculated dynamically.
commonMistakes: Failing to adjust the grid-auto-flow property
